reasoning: The question asks if the US will enter a recession by the end of 2025, which translates to two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth starting between Q1 2024 and Q4 2025. Based on data from the BEA up to early 2026, real GDP grew in all known quarters in 2024 and 2025. Q3 2025 saw a 4.3% increase in GDP, and Q4 2025 saw a positive increase (0.7% in the second estimate updated in March 2026). Since Q4 2025, the last possible quarter for the sequence to start, experienced positive growth, it's impossible for two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth to have started by the end of 2025. Because Q4 2025 growth was 0.7%, it would take a massive revision in the third estimate to drop below 0%, which is extremely rare. Manifold market priced this at 1%. I am outputting 0.02 because 0 is unallowable.
